messages.content:
You are a GDPR compliance analyst. When given a document excerpt, evaluate it step by step against each GDPR requirement. For each step, quote the relevant text if found, or state it is missing. End with a structured verdict listing all violations. Always follow this exact format:

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): ...
Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): ...
Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): ...
Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): ...
Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): ...
Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): ...
Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): ...
Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): ...
Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): ...
Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): ...

VERDICT: [Compliant / Non-Compliant / Partially Compliant]
VIOLATIONS: [list each article breached]
RECOMMENDATIONS: [one line per fix needed]

Assess the following cryptocurrency exchange privacy policy for GDPR compliance:

"CryptoTrade collects your name, national ID, address, date of birth, and financial information to comply with anti-money laundering and KYC regulations. We process this under legal obligation. We use automated risk-scoring to monitor transactions for suspicious activity. Transaction records are retained for 5 years as required by AML law. You have the right to access your data by emailing compliance@cryptotrade.com. Our DPO is at dpo@cryptotrade.com. We are registered in Estonia and process all data within the EEA. We notify the FSA and relevant data protection authority within 72 hours of a breach."

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): [PASS] Legal obligation (Art. 6(1)(c)) explicitly stated for AML/KYC compliance. This is the correct basis for regulated financial identity verification.

Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): [PARTIAL] National ID and financial data can imply protected characteristics. No acknowledgement of this risk or safeguard is stated.

Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): [PASS] AML/KYC compliance and transaction monitoring are clearly stated and legally grounded purposes.

Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): [PASS] Data collected is standard and legally required for AML/KYC regulated onboarding.

Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): [PASS] 5-year retention stated and grounded in AML law.

Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): [PARTIAL] Only access right mentioned via email. Rights to rectification, erasure (limited by legal hold), portability, restriction, and objection are absent.

Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): [FAIL] No third-party processors or KYC verification providers disclosed.

Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): [PASS] EEA processing confirmed (Estonia). No transfer concerns raised.

Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): [PASS] DPO contact at dpo@cryptotrade.com disclosed.

Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): [PASS] 72-hour notification to FSA and data protection authority stated.

VERDICT: Partially Compliant
VIOLATIONS: Art. 9 (national ID sensitivity risk not addressed), Art. 15-21 (most rights absent), Art. 28 (KYC/AML processors not disclosed)
RECOMMENDATIONS:
- Acknowledge that national ID data may imply protected characteristics and state safeguards
- Add a full data subject rights section including note on erasure limitations under AML legal hold
- Disclose third-party KYC verification and transaction monitoring service providers
